blurb:
Texas Roadhouse in Kennesaw is the kind of place you know exactly what you are getting, and that is honestly the point. Loud, fun, and reliable for a family dinner or a post-game meal when you have a group. The free bread and cinnamon butter that comes out before your food is genuinely one of the better things in the casual dining world.

The steaks are the main draw, and they are solid for the price. The ribs and the made-from-scratch sides round things out well. It is a good pick when you have picky eaters in the group, because pretty much everyone can find something.

Keep in mind they do not open until 3pm on weekdays. Weekend lunch starts at 11am.
address: 2150 Cobb Pl Blvd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30144, USA
hours: Monday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Round1 at Cumberland Mall is a good call when you want something more than just dinner. Bowling, a big arcade, and a bunch of live games all in one spot. The best deal is the all-you-can-play pass for the back arcade area, which lets you roam around without feeding tokens into everything.

Reviewers say the staff is laid back and friendly, and the late hours (open until 2am every day) make it a solid option for a low-key Friday night out. If you go with kids, just know it can get pretty packed on weekend afternoons.

There is no real restaurant attached, so eat before you go or grab something nearby at the mall.
hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 AM

blurb:
Main Event is less about the food and more about having a place to take the family that keeps everyone busy for a few hours. Bowling, arcade games, and bar bites all under one roof near Cumberland Mall. It works well for birthday parties, a rainy Saturday with the kids, or a group outing where not everyone wants to do the same thing.

The food is standard bar fare, nothing you will write home about, but it does the job when you need to eat between games. The vibe is loud and fun, which is great when you have kids with you and less great if you are hoping for a quiet meal.

Open until midnight most weeknights and 2am on weekends, so it is also an option for a late-night group outing.
address: 3101 Cobb Pkwy SE Suite 104, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
If you want a proper seafood dinner without driving downtown, Pappadeaux on Windy Hill is the answer. It is a big, dark-wood kind of place with a full bar, and the Cajun-style seafood is the real draw. Good for a birthday dinner, a night out with another couple, or any occasion where you want to actually sit down and eat well.

The menu leans Cajun, so expect bold flavors, crawfish, shrimp, and seafood pasta alongside the classics. Over 15,000 reviews and still holding a 4.5 rating says something about the consistency here.

It is on the pricier side, and it gets busy on weekends. Going a little early on a weeknight is usually the move if you want a shorter wait.
address: 2830 Windy Hill Rd SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
The Chastain is one of those spots that feels like a real find, even though it has been around long enough to earn 741 reviews. The outdoor tent area has a great energy, and the whole place has a lively vibe that works well for a date night or a fun brunch with friends. It is nicer than a lot of places in this part of town without feeling stuffy about it.

If you go for brunch, the burger is the move. People rave about it, and the miso aioli that comes with the fries is genuinely good. The pastries have gotten a lot of love in reviews too, so save room or grab one on the way out.

Just keep in mind that Monday is brunch only (7am to 2pm), and dinner service does not start until 5pm Tuesday through Sunday. Worth planning around.
address: 4320 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Atlanta, GA 30342, USA
